title: pavonia friesii
intro: Pavonia friesii, also known as the Mexican Rose Mallow, is a flowering plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). Traditionally, the leaves and roots of Pavonia friesii have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Mexico, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Pavonia friesii can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
